perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS the Province of Nova Scotia (the “Province”) and Halifax Regional Municipality (the “HRM”) hereinafter referred to as the “ Receiving or Disclosing Party” or “Parties”, may be facilitating the potential transaction of business related to the provision of SAP Services by the Province to the HRM where each Party may disclose certain confidential information to each other.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS the Province of Nova Scotia (the “Province”) and Halifax Regional Municipality (the “HRM”) hereinafter referred to as the “ Disclosing Party” or “Parties”, may be facilitating the potential transaction of business related to the provision of SAP Services by the Province to the HRM where each Party may disclose certain confidential information to each other.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the definition of "Receiving or Disclosing Party" to only "Disclosing Party" creates an in-text contradiction. This contradicts the fundamental premise of a mutual non-disclosure agreement where both parties are expected to both receive and disclose information. The immediate impact is in the 'WHEREAS' clause itself, because both parties are "Receiving or Disclosing Party", but now the HRM has been removed from that category in the definition, so it suggests they only disclose the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS the Province of Nova Scotia (the “Province”) and Halifax Regional Municipality (the “HRM”) hereinafter referred to as the “ Disclosing Party” or “Parties”, may be facilitating the potential transaction of business related to the provision of SAP Services by the Province to the HRM where each Party may disclose certain confidential information to each other.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of "Receiving or Disclosing Party" to only "Disclosing Party". This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text, which is the same text as the changed text, relies on both parties being able to receive information. By removing the 'Receiving' aspect from the definition, it contradicts the understanding that both parties may receive confidential information. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition provided, as it misaligns the fundamental purpose of the agreement where both parties exchange information mutually.

perturbation.original_text: The confidential, proprietary and trade secret information of the Disclosing Party (hereinafter "Confidential Information") provided hereunder is any and all information, in whatever form (including electronic data) that will be disclosed to facilitate the potential transaction of business (related to the provision of SAP Services to HRM) between the Parties, including, but not limited to, portions or parts of the November 1, 2012 Agreement (including any schedules or appendices) between the Province of Nova Scotia and IBM, each Party's disclosure of intellectual property, techniques, sketches, drawings, models, inventions, know-how, processes, apparatus, equipment, algorithms, software programs, software source documents, and formulae related to the current, future and proposed products, documents and services, research, experimental work, development, design details and specifications, engineering, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing, manufacturing, licensing, sales or service customer lists, business forecasts, sales and merchandizing, and marketing plans and information.
perturbation.changed_text: The confidential, proprietary and trade secret information of the Disclosing Party (hereinafter "Private Information") provided hereunder is any and all information, in whatever form (including electronic data) that will be disclosed to facilitate the potential transaction of business (related to the provision of SAP Services to HRM) between the Parties, including, but not limited to, portions or parts of the November 1, 2012 Agreement (including any schedules or appendices) between the Province of Nova Scotia and IBM, each Party's disclosure of intellectual property, techniques, sketches, drawings, models, inventions, know-how, processes, apparatus, equipment, algorithms, software programs, software source documents, and formulae related to the current, future and proposed products, documents and services, research, experimental work, development, design details and specifications, engineering, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing, manufacturing, licensing, sales or service customer lists, business forecasts, sales and merchandizing, and marketing plans and information.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Confidential Information" with "Private Information" in Section 1 creates a contradiction because the rest of the agreement refers to "Confidential Information", while this section now defines the scope using the new term 'Private Information'. This impacts the entire agreement where the obligations are defined for Confidential Information but it's no longer defined here.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees that Confidential Information shall be used only for the purposes of facilitating the business relationship between the Parties. Additionally, subject to section 4 below, the Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall maintain the Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care that the Receiving Party uses to protect its own similar categories of confidential and proprietary information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care under the circumstances. The Receiving Party shall not make any copies of Confidential Information except as necessary to perform hereunder. Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by such Party, its employees, agents, officials, representatives or consultants acting within the scope of their engagement or employment.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information', while the changed text redefines it as 'Private Information'. The 'Contradicted Text' refers specifically to 'Confidential Information' and outlines the Receiving Party's obligations regarding its use, disclosure, and protection. Since the Changed Text changes the defined term, the receiving party's obligations are now undefined for what was originally intended, creating a contradiction in terms within the document.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that Confidential Information shall be used only for the purposes of facilitating the business relationship between the Parties. Additionally, subject to section 4 below, the Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall maintain the Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care that the Receiving Party uses to protect its own similar categories of confidential and proprietary information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care under the circumstances.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that Confidential Data shall be used only for the purposes of facilitating the business relationship between the Parties. Additionally, subject to section 4 below, the Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Data to any third party without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall maintain the Confidential Data with at least the same degree of care that the Receiving Party uses to protect its own similar categories of confidential and proprietary information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care under the circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Confidential Data" in Section 3 introduces inconsistency. The agreement uses both terms without defining 'Confidential Data', creating ambiguity regarding the scope of confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The confidential, proprietary and trade secret information of the Disclosing Party (hereinafter "Confidential Information") provided hereunder is any and all information, in whatever form (including electronic data) that will be disclosed to facilitate the potential transaction of business (related to the provision of SAP Services to HRM) between the Parties, including, but not limited to, portions or parts of the November 1, 2012 Agreement (including any schedules or appendices) between the Province of Nova Scotia and IBM, each Party's disclosure of intellectual property, techniques, sketches, drawings, models, inventions, know-how, processes, apparatus, equipment, algorithms, software programs, software source documents, and formulae related to the current, future and proposed products, documents and services, research, experimental work, development, design details and specifications, engineering, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing, manufacturing, licensing, sales or service customer lists, business forecasts, sales and merchandizing, and marketing plans and information.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Confidential Information,' which is subsequently changed to 'Confidential Data'. The Contradicted Text defines 'Confidential Information' but does not define 'Confidential Data'. This creates a Misaligned Terminology, since there are two terms that are being used interchangeably without a proper definition, leading to conflicting obligations as it is unclear whether 'Confidential Data' falls under the same protections and scope as the defined 'Confidential Information,' thus leading to a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will terminate on the date and time the Agreement between the Parties over the provision of SAP Services is terminated. In the event this Agreement is terminated, and the Disclosing Party so requests, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy (and certify destruction of) all Confidential Information which it received from the Disclosing Party along with all copies. The obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement or the return of the Confidential Information to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will terminate on the date and time the Agreement between the Parties over the provision of SAP Services is completed. In the event this Agreement is terminated, and the Disclosing Party so requests, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy (and certify destruction of) all Confidential Information which it received from the Disclosing Party along with all copies. The obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement or the return of the Confidential Information to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "is terminated" with "is completed" introduces a contradiction in section 9. 'Terminated' implies a deliberate ending of the agreement, while 'completed' suggests it fulfilled its purpose. This ambiguity creates uncertainty about when the confidentiality obligations cease and if returning information is necessary if the SAP services agreement is 'completed' rather than terminated. The agreement will terminate when the "Agreement between the Parties over the provision of SAP Services is completed", this contradicts and introduces in-text contradiction as it does not mean the same as terminate, where the agreement can be finished due to a different cause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will terminate on the date and time the Agreement between the Parties over the provision of SAP Services is completed. In the event this Agreement is terminated, and the Disclosing Party so requests, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy (and certify destruction of) all Confidential Information which it received from the Disclosing Party along with all copies. The obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement or the return of the Confidential Information to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "is terminated" with "is completed" in the agreement termination clause. This introduces a contradiction because 'terminated' implies a deliberate ending, while 'completed' suggests fulfillment of purpose. The contradicted text which is the same as the changed text, suffers from this ambiguity, creating uncertainty about when confidentiality obligations cease, directly contradicting the original intent and leading to potential disputes regarding obligations after the SAP services agreement concludes. The definition of misaligned terminology fits this case.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties acknowledge and understand that all documentation and information , including Confidential Information, provided to the Province of Nova Scotia or HRM, as the case may be, pursuant to this Agreement shall be kept confidential, subject to the provisions of the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act , Province of Nova Scotia, and such other laws of the Province of Nova Scotia and Canada which may apply from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties acknowledge and understand that all documentation and information , including Confidential Details, provided to the Province of Nova Scotia or HRM, as the case may be, pursuant to this Agreement shall be kept confidential, subject to the provisions of the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act , Province of Nova Scotia, and such other laws of the Province of Nova Scotia and Canada which may apply from time to time.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Confidential Details" in Section 10 without defining 'Confidential Details' creates an ambiguity. The document uses "Confidential Information" throughout, so this change brings uncertainty about whether "Confidential Details" falls under the same obligations. The agreement uses both terms without defining 'Confidential Details', creating ambiguity regarding the scope of confidentiality obligations. This contradicts the previous sections, where "Confidential Information" was already being reffered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The confidential, proprietary and trade secret information of the Disclosing Party (hereinafter "Confidential Information") provided hereunder is any and all information, in whatever form (including electronic data) that will be disclosed to facilitate the potential transaction of business (related to the provision of SAP Services to HRM) between the Parties, including, but not limited to, portions or parts of the November 1, 2012 Agreement (including any schedules or appendices) between the Province of Nova Scotia and IBM, each Party's disclosure of intellectual property, techniques, sketches, drawings, models, inventions, know-how, processes, apparatus, equipment, algorithms, software programs, software source documents, and formulae related to the current, future and proposed products, documents and services, research, experimental work, development, design details and specifications, engineering, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing, manufacturing, licensing, sales or service customer lists, business forecasts, sales and merchandizing, and marketing plans and information.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Confidential Information" to "Confidential Details" introduces an ambiguity. The contradicted text defines "Confidential Information", and the changed text introduces a new term, "Confidential Details", without defining it. This misalignment of terminology creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether "Confidential Details" is intended to be covered by the same definition as "Confidential Information," leading to uncertainty about the scope of confidentiality obligations within the document. This directly aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition provided.
